_comment: Workshop Intro Chapter 03 — Extensions & Recovering from Loops. 6 scenes, ~155 words. Single-stem rendering: scenes joined with break tags, one ElevenLabs call. Visual concerns (sourceStart, clipDurationSeconds, label, title) live in src/WorkshopIntroCh03.tsx — this file is voice-generation only.

scenes.text: Inside the VS Code extensions panel, the Claude Code extension lives at the top of your installed list. Two controls matter — Restart, and Disable.
Restart cycles the extension if Claude Code stops responding. Disable turns it off entirely without uninstalling — useful when something's interfering and you want to test in isolation.

When Claude Code does get stuck mid-loop, restarting is the first move. If that's not enough, start a fresh conversation and ask the new instance to read the JSONL session files in your terminal. Those files capture every action — your recovery surface.
Better discipline than recovering after the fact — keep a master sessions log that mirrors the work as it happens. If a session dies, your context doesn't.
Beyond Claude Code, a few extensions earn their slot — image preview for inline screenshots, and a markdown reader hookup for Typora.
When you're scouting more extensions, search the marketplace with at-popular — it surfaces what the rest of the field is using. Install whatever earns its keep.
